metadata.title: US Port Stevedoring Rate Benchmarks
metadata.rate_basis: USD per short ton — labour + equipment combined unless noted
notes: Rates are Gulf Coast benchmarks. East/West Coast typically 10-20% higher.
Equipment costs calculated separately as rate_per_hour × estimated_hours.
Overtime applies at overtime_pct above base rate for night/weekend work.
Throughput rate (TPH) used to estimate berth time for dockage calculation.

commodity_notes.grain: US grain elevators typically include all stevedoring in elevator tariff — verify separately
commodity_notes.coal: Lamberts Point and DTA (Hampton Roads) have their own published tariff schedules
commodity_notes.cement: Pneumatic unloader is owned by either terminal or receiver — verify who supplies
commodity_notes.phosphate: Mosaic and CF Industries publish own tariffs for their terminals
commodity_notes.steel: Rate varies significantly by product form — coils vs. plate vs. rebar vs. wire rod
commodity_notes.liquid_bulk: Pumping charges usually separate from stevedoring — terminal-specific

verification_sources.grain_elevators: Bunge, ADM, LDC, Cargill, CHS individual elevator rate sheets
verification_sources.coal_terminals: Lamberts Point (NS), DTA, Alabama McDuffie — published terminal tariffs
verification_sources.phosphate: Mosaic terminal tariffs at Port Manatee and elsewhere
verification_sources.cement: Individual terminal agreements — not typically published publicly
verification_sources.general: AAPA Seaport Statistics for benchmark validation

grain_elevator.notes: Grain elevator loading rate — high throughput. Elevator may include separate elevation charges.

coal_grab.notes: Grab crane discharge. Lamberts Point (NS): check published tariff. DTA: similar rates.

cement_pneumatic.notes: Pneumatic unloader discharge to silo. Equipment is often receiver-owned — verify who charges.

cement_grab.notes: Grab crane to hopper, then conveyor to silo. Slower than pneumatic.

fertilizer_grab.notes: Fertilizer — hygroscopic cargo, rate includes sheeting/covering during ops.

phosphate_grab.notes: Mosaic Port Manatee and CF Industries publish own terminal tariffs — check directly.

bulk_dry_general.notes: General dry bulk fallback rate when commodity-specific rate not available.

steel_coils.notes: Coil cradles, shore crane. Coil weight typically 20-25 MT each. Rate per ST.

steel_plate.notes: Shore crane with spreader beams. Plate typically faster throughput than coils.

steel_rebar.notes: Bundle discharge with shore crane. Faster than plate due to bundle standardization.

breakbulk_general.notes: Ship's gear or mobile crane. Labor-intensive. Rate varies significantly by package type.

project_cargo.notes: Engineered rigging plans required. Rate highly variable — quote-specific.

liquid_bulk.notes: Shore manifold connection. Pumping charges and heating charges separate. Vapour recovery fee may apply.

containers.rate_basis_note: Container rates typically quoted per TEU (~$200/TEU) not per ton
containers.notes: Rate-per-ton is approximation assuming ~16 tons/TEU. Use per-TEU rate for accuracy.

gulf_coast.note: Base rates above are Gulf Coast benchmarks

east_coast_mid.note: Baltimore, Norfolk, Delaware — higher labor costs

northeast.note: NY/NJ, Boston — significantly higher ILA labor rates

pacific_coast.note: ILWU labor — higher rates than Gulf ILA
